name: Telegram MCP Server (chaindead)
what_it_does: Community MCP server for Telegram enabling AI agents to send messages, create polls, manage bot interactions, and access Telegram Bot API functionality for messaging automation.
use_cases: Sending alerts and notifications to Telegram chats from agents
Delivering agent reports and summaries via Telegram
Managing Telegram group messages in agent workflows
Creating Telegram polls and surveys programmatically
Reading incoming messages for trigger-based agent workflows
not_for: High-throughput messaging at scale (Telegram Bot API has limits)
Accessing private account messages (Bot API only, not user API)
best_when: An agent needs to send notifications, alerts, or reports to Telegram users or groups — especially in technical/developer communities.
avoid_when: You need enterprise messaging with audit logs, GDPR compliance controls, or access to user (non-bot) message history.

auth.notes: Single bot token grants full access to bot functionality. No scope granularity — token is all-or-nothing. Obtain from BotFather on Telegram.

pricing.free_tier_limits: Telegram Bot API is completely free with no request limits for reasonable use
pricing.paid_tiers: Telegram Premium for users; not relevant for bots
pricing.notes: Completely free for bot API usage. No rate-based pricing.

known_agent_gotchas: Bot can only send to chats it has been added to — cannot initiate conversations with new users
30 messages/second global limit shared across all your bots with same token
Group messages limited to 20/minute — notify user if flooding needs to be prevented
No scope granularity — token grants full bot access; no way to restrict to send-only
Community MCP server — may not cover all Telegram Bot API methods
No idempotency — network retry can send duplicate messages
agent_readiness.security_notes: Bot token is single-factor and grants full bot access. No scopes available. Store securely (env var only, never in code). HTTPS enforced by Telegram infrastructure.

security_score_components.security_notes: No scope granularity is the main security gap. Single token = full bot access. HTTPS enforced. Community MCP — verify code and dependencies.
